verdict_reasoning: SRC (p60-Src) is canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation plus a polybasic cluster — its kinase body faces the cytoplasm. However, a 2026 Science study demonstrated that in cancer cells, SRC undergoes autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE) that topologically inverts it onto the outer leaflet ('eSrc'), detectable on cancer cell lines, xenografts, and patient tumor samples. This cancer-cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display is proposed as a therapeutic antibody target. Per the cell_state_induced rule for oncogenic-transformation ecto-display, verdict is contextual rather than no or inner_leaflet_anchored.
